prose:
Method provenance block:
Property: Fukui-reactivity indices (f+ electrophilic susceptibilities, f− nucleophilic susceptibilities, f0 radial function, and dual descriptor) and most reactive sites.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density-fitting (RIJK) in PySCF.
Geometry provenance: fixed `.xyz` supplied by user (path `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz`); no re-optimization – single-point calculations at N, N+1, and N–1 electron counts on this geometry (notation: DFT//user-provided).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation – purely vacuum calculation).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1) for the N-electron state; cation and anion modeled as doublets (charge +1/–1, multiplicity 2) per chemkit default for Fukui analysis.
Software: chemkit front-end invoking PySCF v2.4.0 (backend) through the `fukui-reactivity` skill.
Convergence / caveats: SCFs for all three charge states converged; integrity.status = "ok" and integrity.trustworthy = true. Engine produced three WARNINGS (verbatim below). Density fitting (RIJK) is an additional approximation. Mulliken charge partitioning was employed; Fukui indices are sensitive to the charge model.

Headline result
Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C, index 2 in 0-based counting) with f⁺ = 2.2975867 (dimensionless).
Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (N, index 0) with f⁻ = 0.20452359.

The full per-atom f⁺/f⁻/f⁰ arrays, dual descriptor, charged Mulliken populations, and a PNG visualization are available in the result JSON and in the plot file reported by the engine (`.../agent_call_01_plot.png`).

Live log path
During execution the PySCF/chemkit output streamed in real time to the standard `.out` log created by the engine in the working directory (path echoed by chemkit at launch). Please consult that file for SCF iterations and any additional diagnostic messages; it remains on disk alongside `live.json` (full result) for reproducibility.
